prompt: Passwords are stored using md5(password). What is the correct fix?
code_snippet: String hash = md5(password);  // stored in the users table

options.text: Hash the password twice with SHA-1
Use a slow, salted password hash designed for the purpose: bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2

explanation: Fast general-purpose hashes — even SHA-256 — can be brute-forced billions/sec on a GPU. Password storage needs a deliberately slow, salted algorithm (bcrypt/scrypt/Argon2) — the fix for CWE-916. 'Switch to SHA-256' is the classic wrong answer: still far too fast. Encryption is reversible (wrong tool); a static salt is weak.

prompt: Passwords are stored using sha1(password). What is the correct fix?
code_snippet: String hash = sha1(password);  // stored in the users table

options.text: Use a slow, salted password hash designed for the purpose: bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2
Hash the password twice with SHA-1
Prepend the username as a static salt

explanation: Fast general-purpose hashes — even SHA-256 — can be brute-forced billions/sec on a GPU. Password storage needs a deliberately slow, salted algorithm (bcrypt/scrypt/Argon2) — the fix for CWE-916. 'Switch to SHA-256' is the classic wrong answer: still far too fast. Encryption is reversible (wrong tool); a static salt is weak.
